title: Emergency fund basics
body: Aim for 3–6 months of essential expenses in cash or near-cash (savings, money market). Start small: automate a weekly transfer until you hit one month, then stretch the goal. Use it only for true emergencies (job loss, medical), not sales or gadgets.

title: 50/30/20 budgeting rule
body: Rough guideline: ~50% needs (housing, food, transport), ~30% wants, ~20% debt payoff + savings. Tune percentages to your city’s rent and income; the point is separating fixed obligations from flexible spending.

title: Pay yourself first
body: Schedule savings right after income arrives (same day as salary). Even 5–10% builds momentum. Automating removes willpower from the equation.

title: Debt avalanche vs snowball
body: Avalanche: pay extra toward highest interest rate first (cheapest overall). Snowball: pay smallest balance first (psychological wins). Pick the one you’ll stick with; consistency beats perfection.

title: Tracking leads to behavior change
body: Recording every expense for a few weeks surfaces leaks (subscriptions, small buys). Review weekly categories—not to shame, but to decide what tradeoffs fit your values.

title: Avoid lifestyle creep
body: When income rises, save at least half of the increase before upgrading wants. Raises disappear quickly if fixed costs absorb them immediately.

title: Subscriptions audit
body: List recurring charges monthly. Cancel or downgrade anything unused for 60 days. Negotiate internet/phone annually when promotions expire.

title: Food spending guardrails
body: Meal prep and grocery lists cut impulse dining. Cap weekly dining-out with a separate envelope or prepaid rule; cooking batches lowers cost per meal.

title: Mindful discretionary spending
body: Use a 24–48h pause rule for nonessential buys above your threshold (e.g. $50). Often desire fades; when it doesn’t, buy intentionally.

title: Income diversification
body: Upskilling and small side projects cushion income shocks better than cutting coupons alone. Prioritize skills that compound (certifications in your field).

title: Couples and shared money
body: Align on fixed bills vs flexible money. Joint visibility on totals prevents drift; agree how often you sync (weekly quick review works).

title: Insurance priorities
body: Prioritize health + disability where relevant, then renter/home + adequate liability. Avoid overlapping duplicate policies; shop quotes every 1–2 years.
